# InfraMosaic — Record-Retention Schedule

**Policy version:** 1.0 · **as_of:** 2026-06-13 · **Framework:** IOSCO PRA principles + ISAE 3000  
**Generated by** `build/build_assurance.py` — version-stamped; update by editing the generator, not this file.

---

## 1. Scope

This schedule sets retention periods, storage locations and immutability mechanisms per record class. It implements the IOSCO PRA *document / record retention* principle.

## 2. Retention table

| Record class | Retention | Storage | Immutability mechanism |
|---|---|---|---|
| Source data CSVs (`data/*.csv`) | Indefinite | git repository | git history + per-build sha256 in every ledger record |
| Scrape corpus (`scrape/`) | Indefinite | git repository | git history + extractor sha256 in claim records |
| Ledger snapshots (`ledger/publication_ledger.json`) | Indefinite | git repository | hash chain (`content_hash` per record) |
| Ledger hash archive (`ledger/ledger_hash_archive.json`) | Indefinite | git repository | append-only accumulation of every published hash |
| Corrections (`ledger/corrections_log.json`) | min. 7 years (floor 5) | git repository | independent append-only hash chain |
| Complaints records | min. 5 years | corrections log + complaints file | append-only; supersedes-hash |
| Approvals (`governance/approvals.json`) | Indefinite | git repository | append-only; per-entry `entry_hash` |
| Methodology registry (`methodology/registry.json`) | Indefinite | git repository | per-method `method_sha256` + append-only changelog |

## 3. Immutability

Retention is **genuinely enforced**, not merely stated: git history preserves every prior state, and the publication hash chain + the independent corrections chain + the append-only hash archive make source CSVs, ledger snapshots, corrections and approvals tamper-evident. A retroactive edit to any retained record breaks a hash and is caught by `build/build_ledger.py --verify`, `build/corrections.py verify`, or the test suite.

## 4. Honest status

This control is recorded as **MET**: the retention periods are defined per class and the immutability mechanism is real and re-verifiable today.
